H.264 NAL type 1 is Non-IDR slice (P-frame) with hex headers 0x21, 0x41, 0x61.
H.264 NAL type 5 is IDR slice (keyframe) with hex headers 0x25, 0x45, 0x65.
H.264 NAL type 7 is SPS (Sequence Parameter Set) with hex headers 0x27, 0x47, 0x67.
H.264 NAL type 8 is PPS (Picture Parameter Set) with hex headers 0x28, 0x48, 0x68.
NAL type is extracted with nalType = headerByte & 0x1F.

A correct video packet log starts with the 00 00 00 01 start code (e.g. "Video packet #1: 3849 bytes, first 16: 00 00 00 01 27 64 00 32...").
An incorrect video packet (wrong header stripping) is missing the start code, e.g. "Video packet #1: 3861 bytes, first 16: 67 FF 7F 01 3E 05 93 00...".
Audio can be checked via adb logcat -s CARLINK:V | grep -E "Audio packet|Injecting audio".

claims.statement: The SendFile USB header is 16 bytes.
The USB header magic value is 0x55AA55AA.
The USB header contains a PayloadLen field.
The SendFile message type value is 0x00000099 (type=153).
The USB header type check field for SendFile is 0xFFFFFF66.
The SendFile payload structure consists of PathLen (4B LE), FilePath (N bytes + null), ContentLen (4B LE), and FileContent (M bytes).
claims.quote:
| PathLen  | FilePath          | ContentLen | FileContent       |
| (4B LE)  | (N bytes + null)  | (4B LE)    | (M bytes)         |

claims.statement: The pathLen field is at offset 0x00, size 4 bytes, and holds the length of the file path string excluding null.
claims.quote: | pathLen | 0x00 | 4 | Length of file path string (excluding null) |

claims.statement: The filePath field is at offset 0x04, size N+1 bytes, and is a null-terminated absolute path on the adapter.
claims.quote: | filePath | 0x04 | N+1 | Null-terminated file path (absolute path on adapter) |

claims.statement: The contentLen field is at offset 0x04+N+1, size 4 bytes, and holds the length of file content in bytes.
claims.quote: | contentLen | 0x04+N+1 | 4 | Length of file content in bytes |

claims.statement: The content field is at offset 0x04+N+5, size M bytes, holding raw file content bytes.
claims.quote: | content | 0x04+N+5 | M | Raw file content bytes |

claims.statement: The total SendFile payload length is 4 + (N+1) + 4 + M = N + M + 9 bytes.

When a filename matches 'Update.img', the firmware triggers an OTA update by executing system("/script/update_box_ota.sh <path> &").
The standard recommended update path is /tmp/<model>_Update.img.
An example update path for the CPC200 model is /tmp/CPC200_Update.img.

claims.statement: Method B trigger uses D-Bus with the HUD command string HUDComand_A_UploadFile to trigger file processing.

claims.statement: In the SendFile message, path_len is computed excluding the null terminator and content_len is the length of firmware data.
The USB header type_check is computed as msg_type XOR 0xFFFFFFFF.
The USB header is packed as four little-endian uint32 fields: magic, payload length, msg_type, type_check.
claims.quote: header = struct.pack('<IIII', magic, len(payload), msg_type, type_check)

claims.statement: After sending, the update executes /script/update_box_ota.sh /tmp/CPC200_Update.img &.
In the TypeScript implementation, the payload buffer is allocated as 4 + pathBuffer.length + 4 + contentLen bytes.
In the TypeScript header, magic 0x55AA55AA is written at offset 0, payload length at offset 4, type 0x99 at offset 8, and type check 0xFFFFFF66 at offset 12.
claims.quote:
header.writeUInt32LE(0x55AA55AA, 0);              // Magic
  header.writeUInt32LE(payload.length, 4);          // Length
  header.writeUInt32LE(0x99, 8);                    // Type (SendFile)
  header.writeUInt32LE(0xFFFFFF66, 12);             // Type check

In firmware 2025.10.15, update status code 6 means Success/complete.
In firmware 2025.10.15, update status code 7 means Error/failed.

ssh root@192.168.43.1

claims.statement: RFCOMM message Type 6 (P→A) is WifiConnectStatus, WiFi connection result.
RFCOMM message Type 7 (P→A) is WifiStartResponse, WiFi streaming ready.

Wireless AA WiFi negotiation flow: Adapter sends Type 4 InitInfo; phone sends Type 5 WifiVersionResponse; adapter sends Type 1 WifiVersionRequest; phone sends Type 2 WifiInfoRequest; adapter sends Type 3 WifiInfo (SSID, password, IP, port); phone sends Type 7 WifiStartResponse; then WiFi connects; phone sends Type 6 WifiConnectStatus=0.
Wireless Android Auto WiFi streaming begins on TCP port 54321.
WifiInfo (Type 3) field channelfreq example value 5180 is the WiFi channel frequency in MHz.
WifiInfo (Type 3) field channeltype example value 0.
WifiInfo (Type 3) field ip example value 192.168.43.1 is the adapter's IP address.
WifiInfo (Type 3) field port example value 54321 is the TCP port for AA streaming.
WifiInfo (Type 3) field ssid example value CarPlay-Adapter is the WiFi network name.
WifiInfo (Type 3) field passwd example value 12345678 is the WiFi password.
WifiInfo (Type 3) field bssid example value DE:AD:BE:EF:00:02 is the adapter's WiFi MAC.
WifiInfo (Type 3) field securityMode example value 8 corresponds to WPA2-PSK.
TTY log shows BoxRFCOMMService initWifiInfo with channelfreq 5180, channeltype 0, ip 192.168.43.1, port 54321, ssid CarPlay-Adapter, passwd 12345678, bssid DE:AD:BE:EF:00:02, securityMode 8.
